sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The DOWN action moved the agent. The animation_changed_bbox is [8, 14, 63, 36], which spans from rows 8-63, cols 14-36. This suggests the agent moved DOWN significantly. Let me check the agent's current position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check agent position
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] > 5:
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check maze around agent
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nRows 10-20, cols 12-22:")
for i in range(10, 21):
    row = ""
    for j in range(12, 23):
        ch = ascii_lines[i][j]
        if ch == 'W':
            row += '.'
        elif ch == 'B':
            row += '#'
        elif ch == 'R':
            row += 'R'
        elif ch == 'b':
            row += '@'
        else:
            row += '?'
    print(f"Row {i:2d}: {row}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The agent moved DOWN from rows 8-12, cols 14-18 to rows 14-18, cols 14-18. This is within the left vertical bar. The agent can continue moving DOWN.
Let me check the ascii at rows 19-30, cols 13-19:
Row 19: ########### (black wall)
Row 20: ########### (black wall)
...
Row 25: ########### (black wall)
Row 26: ##............. (white passages)
So the black area at rows 19-25, cols 13-31 is continuous. The agent can move DOWN from rows 14-18, cols 14-18 to rows 19-23, cols 14-18, then to rows 24-28, cols 14-18.
But rows 26-28, cols 14-18 is white passages. So the agent can't move DOWN from rows 24-28, cols 14-18.
Wait, but earlier the agent was at rows 32-36, cols 14-18, which is below rows 26-28. So there must be a way for the agent to move DOWN through the gap at rows 26-28.
Let me check the ascii at rows 25-30, cols 13-19:
Row 25: ########### (black wall)
Row 26: ##............. (white passages)
Row 27: ##............. (white passages)
Row 28: ##............. (white passages)
Row 29: ##............. (white passages)
Row 30: .#######... (black wall)
So rows 26-29, cols 13-14 is ## (black wall), and cols 15-19 is ..... (white passages). Row 30, cols 14-20 is ####### (black wall).
This means there's a gap in the black area at rows 26-29, cols 15-19. The agent can't move through this gap.
But the agent was at rows 32-36, cols 14-18, which is below the gap. So the agent must have moved DOWN through the gap somehow.
Wait, maybe the agent's movement is not constrained by the black area. Maybe the agent can move through white passages, but it's only "valid" when it's within the black area.
Let me try moving DOWN multiple times to see if the agent can pass through the gap.
